title: elaeagnus pungens f. aurea
intro: Elaeagnus pungens f. aurea, also known as the golden sea buckthorn, is a variety of the Elaeagnus pungens shrub, native to China and widely cultivated for its ornamental and medicinal value. This plant is known for its bright yellow foliage and ability to thrive in poor soil conditions, making it a popular choice for landscaping. The golden sea buckthorn has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Elaeagnus pungens f. aurea can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or oil, and is often used to support cardiovascular health, improve digestion, and promote overall well-being.
